_meta.purpose: Source of truth for every reusable component in /components. An agent generating a new page must check this file first.
_meta.rule: If a page needs something not listed here, do NOT invent a new one-off component that duplicates an existing pattern. Instead, flag it as a "component gap": name the gap, describe what's needed, and point to the closest existing component it might extend. Record the gap in this file's "_gaps" array (add one if it doesn't exist yet) rather than silently building something ad hoc.
_meta.scope_note: This covers /components only. Page templates that compose several of these (e.g. CaseStudyPage) are included too, tagged category: "page_template", since a future agent needs to know they exist before building a competing template from scratch.
known_inconsistencies: Two near-identical blues in use: color.brand.accent (#004aeb, the 'real' token) and color.brand.highlight (#0658FC, used only on /explorations and the blog author box). Unresolved — see tokens.json.

Footer is currently disabled site-wide: both call sites (app/page.tsx and CaseStudyPage.tsx) have it commented out behind a 'TODO: re-enable when ready' note. It is documented below as if active since the component itself is intact and unchanged; check those call sites before assuming it renders anywhere.

Navbar.description: Sticky full-width top bar: logo, primary nav links (desktop only), mobile hamburger (MobileMenu). Rendered once, globally, in app/layout.tsx.
variants: none — singleton, rendered once for the whole site

states.default: Transparent background, at the top of an unscrolled page.
states.link:active_route: text-white (matches hover color) + aria-current="page", determined via usePathname().
states.link:disabled: href: null renders a static, non-interactive text-white/30 span instead of a Link.

Navbar.when_to_use: Never render directly on a page — it already lives in the root layout and appears on every route automatically.
do_not: Don't add a second Navbar instance on any page.

Don't add a nav link here without also deciding whether it belongs in MobileMenu's link list (they are currently separate, hand-maintained lists — About/Blog placeholders exist commented out in both).

LeftNav.description: Hero sidebar: heading, subheading, TrustedStrip, CtaButtons. Used on the homepage, /call, and /explorations, each passing its own heading/subheading copy.

variants.sticky: boolean prop. true = pinned to the viewport, height calc(100vh-6.5rem) (used with page-level scroll, e.g. homepage, /explorations). false (default) = fills a fixed-height row (used by /call, which has its own internal-scroll shell).
variants.balanceHero: boolean prop, applies text-balance/text-pretty for nicer line wrapping. Off by default because the homepage H1's line breaks were hand-tuned.
variants.headingSize: string prop, Tailwind class override for desktop heading size (default desk:text-[30px]).
variants.headingFontClassName: string prop, extra className applied only to the <h1> — e.g. a next/font className to swap the heading's font on a single page without touching the site-wide font-display.

states.entrance: fade-up animation on mount, staggered via inline animation-delay per child block.

LeftNav.when_to_use: Any page that needs the site's "hero sidebar" pattern: big heading + subheading + trusted-by strip + CTA buttons, paired with a content column to its right.
do_not: Don't confuse this with CaseSidebar — case study pages use a different, data-driven sidebar (client/year/involvement info) instead of this hero copy.
It renders as <aside>, not <nav> — it's hero copy + CTAs, not a navigation link list. Don't wrap it in nav semantics.
Don't hardcode a new hero block elsewhere — extend this component's props instead.

CaseSidebar.description: Case-study-specific sidebar: back button, ClientInfo (client/year/involvement/link), long-form description, CtaButtons. Sticky on desktop.
variants: none — always the same shape, data comes from the CaseStudy object

states.mobile: Renders nothing (client info moves under the title, description hidden) — see comment in file.

CaseSidebar.when_to_use: Case study pages only (components/case-study/CaseStudyPage.tsx), one per page, paired with a <main> content column as a sibling (not nested).
do_not: Don't reuse LeftNav's raw desk:w-[26%] value elsewhere on a page that lacks the homepage's p-5 outer shell — this component's width is desk:w-[calc(26%-10.4px)], deliberately adjusted to render the same effective width as LeftNav on a page without that shell. Reuse this exact formula, not LeftNav's.

Showcase.description: Renders the homepage's project list as the page's <main> landmark. One ProjectCard per project.

variants.scroll: "internal" (default) = column scrolls independently inside a fixed-height shell. "page" = no internal scroll, used on the homepage so the page itself scrolls and the footer can appear below the fold.

states: none — content is static per the projects data
Showcase.when_to_use: Homepage only, currently — it IS the page's <main>, not a generic reusable list container.
do_not: Don't render more than one per page (it's a <main> landmark).
Don't reuse this for a different image set (e.g. explorations) — that page has its own ExplorationGallery instead.

ProjectCard.description: One homepage project entry: media (image or video), optional overlay logo, links out (internal Link or external <a>).
variants: external vs internal link, driven by project.external

states.hover: Media scales up 1.05x via group-hover, clipped by the frame.
states.entrance: fade-up with a staggered delay based on list index (capped at 800ms).

tokens_used: radius: none (square corners are intentional, not an oversight)
ProjectCard.when_to_use: Inside Showcase only, one per project.
do_not: Don't add border-radius/shadow — square corners are a deliberate Figma decision.
Don't skip the priority/fetchPriority wiring on the first card — it's the homepage's LCP element.

CtaButtons.description: The paired 'Chat on Telegram' + 'Start Your Project' buttons.

variants.telegramSolidBlack: boolean prop. false (default) = translucent color.neutral.button-dark with a hover lighten (used in LeftNav/CaseSidebar). true = solid black, no hover lighten (used in Footer).

CtaButtons.when_to_use: Anywhere the site's two primary CTAs need to appear together (currently: LeftNav, CaseSidebar, Footer).
do_not: Don't change 'Start Your Project' back into an internal <Link> to /call — it deliberately opens links.discoveryCall (an external Cal.com URL) in a new tab now.
Don't add a third button here — it's built for exactly two, laid out with flex-1 each.

TrustedStrip.description: "Trusted by top YC companies" label + an infinite horizontal marquee of logos (baked strip image + a few extra SVG logos), duplicated once for a seamless loop.
states: continuous CSS marquee animation, no interactive states
do_not: Don't add a logo to only one of the two duplicated blocks — both copies must stay identical or the loop will visibly jump.
The first copy carries real alt text; the duplicate is aria-hidden — keep that split when adding logos.

MobileMenu.description: Hamburger button + dropdown panel (Telegram link, 'Start now' link), mobile only.

states.open: X icon, panel visible below the navbar.

do_not: Don't remove desk:hidden — this is the mobile-only counterpart to Navbar's desktop nav links.
Its own link list is separate from Navbar's navLinks array — update both if adding a destination.

Logo.description: Site wordmark (single SVG, mark + wordmark), links to /.
do_not: Don't change the -ml-[4.7px] negative margin without re-checking the SVG's baked-in left whitespace — it's a deliberate optical alignment fix, not arbitrary.

Footer.description: Site footer: giant Union wordmark, CtaButtons + nav links, copyright/social row. Shared between the homepage and case study pages.
Footer.status: Currently disabled site-wide — both render call sites have it commented out pending a TODO. The component itself is intact.
Footer.when_to_use: Once per page, as the very last element, on pages that scroll to a natural end (currently: homepage, case study pages, when re-enabled).
do_not: Don't re-enable it without checking the background-image blur/z-index fix made for it earlier (Footer needed position: relative so it paints correctly above the page's background-image layers).
Its own <footer> requires relative positioning to stack correctly above absolutely-positioned page background layers — don't remove that.

variants: Display column is hidden on mobile (desk:flex only) — only Filter shows there.
ArticleFilters.when_to_use: Top of /blog only, currently a visual/interactive placeholder — it doesn't yet actually filter the article list below it.
do_not: Don't add a flex `gap` between the two columns without removing their percentage widths (or vice versa) — gap + width summing to 100% overflows the row. Use flex-1 if a gap is needed, matching the fix already applied here.

ExplorationGallery.description: Renders the /explorations image list (full-width and 50/50-pair blocks) plus the Grid/Zoom toggle, plus a click-to-expand lightbox (framer-motion fade/scale, backdrop blur, prev/next arrows, Escape/arrow-key nav, body scroll lock).
variants: blocks prop: { kind: "full", src } | { kind: "pair", srcs: [a, b] }, ordered top-to-bottom exactly as rendered.

states.view:grid: Default. Derived (not separately tracked) from whether the lightbox is closed.
states.view:zoom: Derived from the lightbox being open — clicking 'Zoom' opens it at image index 0; closing the lightbox by any means reverts to 'grid'.
states.lightbox:open/closed: AnimatePresence-driven fade/scale; body overflow locked while open.

do_not: Don't reintroduce a separate `view` state — it's intentionally derived from openIndex so the active button can never desync from the actual lightbox state.
Don't give two side-by-side pair images `w-1/2` plus a `gap` together — same overflow bug as ArticleFilters; use `min-w-0 flex-1` instead.

SectionShell.description: Case study Q&A section wrapper: uppercase heading in the left half, full-width hairline rule, content in the right half (aligned to the vertical center rule).

variants.topRule: boolean. Adds a full-width hairline above the section (used for the FAQ block).

SectionShell.when_to_use: Case study pages only, for the repeated 'heading left / content right' Q&A pattern (including the FAQ block).
do_not: Don't reuse this for a full-width section — it hardcodes a 50/50 desktop split matched to the case study sidebar layout. The blog page's FAQ section deliberately does NOT use this for exactly that reason; it builds its own full-width heading + content stack instead.

CaseFaq.description: FAQ accordion, one item open at a time (first open by default). Each question is an <h3> wrapping the toggle button.

states.item:open: Answer expanded via grid-template-rows animation, opacity 1.
states.item:closed: grid-rows-[0fr], opacity 0, but stays in the DOM (not unmounted).

CaseFaq.when_to_use: Case study pages (via SectionShell) and the /blog page (standalone, full width) — same shared FAQ content (lib/case-studies.ts pixelupFaq) and matching FAQPage JSON-LD schema on both.
do_not: Don't put the heading (<h3>) inside the <button> — headings cannot legally nest inside button elements. It wraps the button instead.

CaseMedia.description: CaseMediaSlot (single image/video frame with tone placeholder fills) + CaseMediaBlock ("full" 1160x696 frame or "pair" of 576x548 frames).

variants.tone: "white" | "faint" | "green" — placeholder fill shown when a slot has no real media yet.

states: priority prop: true = loading="eager" + fetchPriority="high" (the case hero image only); false = lazy.
CaseMedia.when_to_use: Case study pages only, between Q&A sections and as the hero.
do_not: Don't use the deprecated next/image `priority` prop on new usages here — this project is on Next 16, where it no longer implies fetchPriority="high"; use loading + fetchPriority explicitly instead.

CaseSection.description: Renders a CaseStudy section's lead paragraph, focus list, results rows, and/or paragraphs, inside a SectionShell.
variants: content is conditional on which optional fields (lead/focus/results/paragraphs) exist on the section data
CaseSection.when_to_use: Case study pages only, once per CaseSection entry in a CaseStudy's sections array.
do_not: Don't add a new section shape without extending the shared CaseSection type in lib/case-studies.ts first.

ClientInfo.description: Client / Year / Involvement / Link rows with hairline dividers. Lives in CaseSidebar on desktop, under the title on mobile.

MoreProjects.description: "More Projects" section at the end of a case study: two equal-width project cards with an overlay logo/title/tags.
variants: none — refs prop (MoreProjectRef[]) drives which projects show

states.card:hover: Media scales up, same treatment as the homepage ProjectCard.

MoreProjects.when_to_use: End of every case study page.
do_not: Don't give the two cards a flex `gap` together with fixed widths that already sum to 100% — use flex-1, same fix as ArticleFilters/ExplorationGallery.

CaseStudyPage.description: Full case study page template: background image, CaseSidebar + main content column (title, hero, CaseSection/CaseMedia loop, CaseFaq, MoreProjects, Footer).
variants: none — study prop (CaseStudy) drives all content
CaseStudyPage.when_to_use: Rendered once by app/case-studies/[slug]/page.tsx per case study. Also renders the FAQPage JSON-LD schema.
do_not: Don't build a competing case-study template — add a new CaseStudy entry to lib/case-studies.ts instead; this template handles layout for all of them.

CalEmbed.description: Inline Cal.com booking widget (@calcom/embed-react), dark-themed to match the site.
states: none — Cal's own UI handles booking states internally
do_not: Don't change calLink/namespace without updating the matching getCalApi namespace call — they must match.
